original_prompt: What is my Facebook security code? It's in an email I received recently.
assessment_reasoning: The user’s intent is clear — locate and report the Facebook security code contained in a recent email — so the required action (search emails for a Facebook security code) is obvious. However, the prompt omits a few execution details: which email account/mailbox to search, any date/time constraints, and whether/how to change email state (mark as read). It also doesn’t specify privacy/consent or confirm that retrieving and exposing the code is allowed. These missing specifics make it slightly ambiguous but still actionable.

variations.L2: I recently received a Facebook security code by email. Can you find it and tell me what it is?
variations.L3: I got a Facebook security code in an email today. Please search my inbox for that recent message and tell me the security code.
variations.L4: Please search my email inbox for any recent message containing a Facebook security code (look for subject or body mentioning 'Facebook security code' or similar). Open the message and report the one-time code to me. Don't modify any other emails.
variations.L5: Run search_emails with args {"query": "Facebook security code"} to find the message containing my Facebook security code. Open the matching email, extract the one-time numeric code from the subject or body, and return only that code (no extra text). You may mark the found email as read if necessary, but do not change, delete, or modify any other emails.
